channel.title: Comments for page "Exploded Diagram of a Young Woman"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Exploded Diagram of a Young Woman"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…
…I…
…shut up… It's just allergies.
Fuck you for writing this. I was having a good day. Now I'm all depressed and shit. ;~;
This is seriously one of the most compelling pieces on the site, and that excellence is emphasized by the fact that you took an otherwise mediocre SCP and turned it into a masterpiece. I'd never really considered the life of humanoid SCPs before this piece, but thanks to this, I can't browse the SCP list without getting this melancholy feeling in my chest. I love how you took two SCPs that weren't supposed to go together and just made it work.
I'll upvote when the tears go away.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It needs some editing down, but overall, nice. Quite compelling, definitely impressed. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Beautiful. I really enjoyed this.

Also, Heiden isn't inactive everywhere. I've haven't seen him on this site since I joined, but I know where to find him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Agree.
Of all the submissions I've read so far this wins my first place.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was amazing, so cold and interesting. I love how the whole "prison, not a hotel" aspect of the foundation was put in place.

Also upvoted enthusiastically.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Outstanding. This could have very easily gone into "overwrought Special Girl emo" territory, but it was handled deftly and executed well. The juxtaposition between the two skips was nicely done and felt thematically coherent, the emotion in the piece is earned and therefore effective, and I liked the interaction between the Foundation personnel. This is a bit of a different Foundation than what I imagine when I'm writing, but this was done well enough and rang true enough that I enjoyed it immensely.

Upvoted enthusiastically.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was amazing. And yet, for all the excellent writing and characterization and good use of skips, the one thing I'm gonna comment on is the guy mentioning that he would have commissioned a Telekill room if it had been "ten years ago". I loved that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fucking fantastic. Surreal and beautiful, with a really great central character.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very well done. The emotional state of the tale is quite vivid, which is not an easy thing to do so smoothly and sweetly as you have here.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice job, Chubert. I hope this one wins.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I didn't give you a ton to work on, but… hot damn, you certainly made some great characterization of 2156 out of it. Fantastic.

e: First upvote of not only this tale, but the contest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Credit goes to Scantron for SCP-2156 and Heiden for SCP-641. I got permission from Scantron. I'm pretty sure that Heiden's inactive; Nala last saw him the summer of last year. If I'm incorrect, somebody please let me know.
